name: WP Engine Managed WordPress Hosting API
what_it_does: Managed WordPress hosting platform with REST API — enabling WordPress agencies, developers, and enterprises to programmatically manage WordPress sites, staging environments, domains, CDN, and deployments, with API access for automation, site provisioning, and headless WordPress (Atlas) for decoupled WordPress frontends on Next.js or Gatsby.
use_cases: Provisioning WordPress sites via WP Engine API for agency client onboarding automation
Managing staging/production environments via WP Engine API for CI/CD deployment pipelines
Automating domain management via WP Engine API for multi-site WordPress networks
Building headless WordPress sites via Atlas (WP Engine) for React/Next.js frontends
Monitoring WordPress site health via WP Engine API for managed hosting operations
not_for: Low-cost shared hosting alternatives (WP Engine is premium pricing — use SiteGround or Bluehost for budget)
Static site hosting without WordPress CMS (use Netlify or Vercel for pure static sites)
best_when: A WordPress agency or enterprise needs programmatic site management and automated deployments — where WP Engine's API enables CI/CD pipelines, environment provisioning, and automated WordPress fleet management.
avoid_when: You're not using WordPress, need budget shared hosting, or building non-WordPress applications.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with username (WP Engine account username) and password (API key from portal). Base64-encoded credentials in Authorization header. API keys generated in WP Engine User Portal. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhooks for site events. Separate Atlas (headless) API uses different auth. Rate limiting applies per account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; free trial available
pricing.paid_tiers: Startup $20/month (1 site); Professional $40/month (3 sites); Growth $77/month (10 sites); Scale $193/month (30 sites); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. Founded 2010 by Jason Cohen. Raised $250M+ total. Acquired by Silver Lake (private equity) 2018. 120,000+ customers. WordPress-exclusive managed hosting. 99.95% SLA. Global CDN (powered by Cloudflare and MaxCDN). 24/7 WordPress support. Free SSL. Automatic backups. Staging environments on all plans. Genesis framework included (premium WordPress themes). Headless WordPress product: Atlas (Faust.js). WP Engine acquired: Flywheel (2019), StudioPress (2018), Delicious Brains (2022). Prominent WordPress ecosystem company. Acquired by Newfold Digital?

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not fully documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth (username + API key). Webhooks. SOC2/GDPR/PCI-DSS. WordPress-exclusive platform. Good developer documentation. OpenAPI spec at wpengineapi.com. Atlas for headless WordPress (separate API).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Site reads idempotent by install ID. Environment reads idempotent. Domain reads idempotent. Site creation not idempotent — check for existing before creating. Backup creation not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth: username is WP Engine account username, password is API key (not account password)
WordPress-only platform — no way to host non-WordPress applications via WP Engine API
Atlas (headless WordPress) is a separate product with separate API and billing
Install ID (not site name) used as primary identifier in API calls — retrieve from dashboard or API
Rate limiting per account — be careful with automated provisioning scripts
Staging to production push via API — powerful but irreversible without backup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, PCI-DSS. HTTP Basic auth (username + API key) — weaker than OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. API key management in portal. US/EU/AU/APAC data centers. WordPress-specific security: malware scanning, WAF. Auto SSL. Silver Lake PE ownership — enterprise security posture.
